scenario: Tessera SaaS runs 50000 instances per month of its onboarding process on Camunda 8.8 and is mapping each Camunda 8 component to one of the three pillars the docs call out: Design, Automate, and Improve. The platform architect needs to confirm which pillar Optimize specifically supports across the suite.
question: Which of the three Camunda 8 pillars does Optimize specifically support according to the Optimize overview?

options.text: Optimize supports the Improve pillar, leveraging process data captured during execution to analyze bottlenecks and identify areas for ongoing process improvement at the org
Optimize supports the Design pillar, providing BPMN authoring tools that let business analysts model new processes before they reach the Camunda 8 runtime for execution
Optimize supports the Automate pillar, executing service tasks and external job workers on behalf of the Camunda 8 runtime to drive process instances forward end to end
Optimize supports all three pillars equally, replacing Modeler for Design, Zeebe for Automate, and the historical reporting layer for Improve inside a single combined product

a.text: Correct. The Optimize page lists Design, Automate, and Improve as the three pillars and positions Optimize as the Improve component leveraging process data for improvement.

b.text: Incorrect. Design is supported by Modeler, where users author BPMN, DMN, and Form diagrams; Optimize is not described as a process design tool anywhere in the Camunda 8 docs.

c.text: Incorrect. Automate is the runtime layer carried by Zeebe and connectors; Optimize is an analytical layer that reads historical data and does not execute process instances itself.

d.text: Incorrect. Each of the three pillars is supported by distinct Camunda 8 components; Optimize is described specifically as the Improve component, not a replacement for the other two.

explanation: Camunda 8 covers three pillars: Design, Automate, and Improve. Modeler covers Design and Zeebe covers Automate, while Optimize is positioned as the Improve component using historical process data.
